name: Current Affairs in General Science (9): India's nOPV2 Polio Vaccine Gains WHO Prequalification

sourceRefs.label: who.int/polioeradication.org - Biological E Limited's nOPV2 WHO prequalification (12 February 2026)

researchNotes: Ninth Science current-affairs topic, opening Part A's 9th rotation lap. Note: initial research query incorrectly assumed Bharat Biotech was the manufacturer — cross-verified against WHO/GPEI sources and corrected to Biological E Limited before writing, consistent with this project's verify-before-asserting practice. Expansion pass (Batch 21): added what WHO prequalification actually means and why it matters for global procurement.

reading.intro: Covers a 2026 global-health milestone: an Indian manufacturer's novel oral polio vaccine achieving WHO prequalification.

qa.question: Which Indian company's novel oral polio vaccine type 2 (nOPV2) received WHO prequalification, and when?
qa.answer: Biological E Limited (BioE) — prequalified by WHO on 12 February 2026.

qa.question: What technology partnership enabled Biological E's full-scale manufacturing of nOPV2?
qa.answer: A technology transfer from PT Bio Farma (Persero), Indonesia — expanding BioE's role from fill-finish activities (using PT Bio Farma's bulk vaccine) to full in-house bulk manufacturing.

qa.question: How many doses of nOPV2 has Biological E already contributed to the global stockpile, and what is its projected additional annual capacity after this prequalification?
qa.answer: 700 million doses contributed since March 2024; an additional 600 million doses per year projected with the new prequalification.

qa.question: What is nOPV2's significance for global health, and which 2 manufacturers now produce it at full scale?
qa.answer: It is a critical tool for stopping outbreaks of the type-2 variant poliovirus. With this prequalification, Biological E (India) becomes the SECOND full manufacturer of nOPV2, joining PT Bio Farma (Indonesia) — together providing diversified, more stable global production capacity.

qa.question: What is WHO 'prequalification', and why does it matter for a vaccine manufacturer?
qa.answer: A WHO assessment process certifying that a vaccine meets international standards of quality, safety, and efficacy — required before UN agencies (like UNICEF and the GPEI) can procure and distribute it globally. Prequalification effectively opens the door to large-scale international bulk procurement, not just domestic sales.

questions.prompt: Which Indian company's nOPV2 (novel oral polio vaccine type 2) received WHO prequalification on 12 February 2026?
Biological E's nOPV2 manufacturing capability came via a technology transfer from which country's manufacturer?
Since March 2024, Biological E has contributed how many doses of nOPV2 to the global stockpile?
nOPV2 is specifically a tool for stopping outbreaks of which poliovirus variant?
With this WHO prequalification, Biological E becomes the world's ___ full manufacturer of nOPV2.
WHO 'prequalification' of a vaccine primarily enables:
options: Large-scale international bulk procurement by UN agencies
questions.explanation: Large-scale international bulk procurement by UN agencies.
